creator: marklmi26-fbsd@yahoo.com
text:
(In reply to Aleks from comment #9)

I am not a FreeBSD committer and can not directly change anything.

There was an errata for binary-updates:

https://www.freebsd.org/security/advisories/FreeBSD-EN-16:09.freebsd-update.asc

I did not come up with anything specific for a source code based upgrade.

Still, the following might help:

FreeBSD 10.1 went EOL on 2016-Dec-31.

It can be appropriate to upgrade to something released before that
date so that there is a time overlap when going from 10.x to
11.x.

FreeBSD 11.0 was released on 2016-Oct-10.
FreeBSD 11.1 was released on 2017-Jul-26 (too late).

So the latest update that overlaps in time is 11.0.

Going the other way from the final target (11.2):

FreeBSD 11.2 was released on 2018-Jun-28.

But:

FreeBSD 10.3 went EOL on 2018-Apr-30 (too soon).
FreeBSD 10.4 went EOL on 2018-Oct-31.

So you might try updating from 10.1 to 10.4 or to 11.0
and then from there to 11.2, possibly even:
10.1->10.4->11.0->11.2 if it is all done by source
based upgrades.
